skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate, edit, upscale, and stitch AI images through the inference.sh belt CLI using GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and related image models.
skill.summary: Create AI images and image edits with inference.sh model commands.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: The Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown code spans and fenced shell examples, not embedded Ruby execution. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is also a false positive from descriptive text, but the skill legitimately runs a networked CLI that sends prompts and image URLs to external image services. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run belt commands that call hosted image models. This is expected behavior, but prompts and parameters leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands directly invoke external model applications through the belt CLI. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it creates network and usage-cost exposure.

medium_findings.title: External Image URL Processing
medium_findings.description: Several examples pass image URLs into hosted image-editing, upscaling, or stitching apps. Users must avoid private or sensitive images unless the provider terms allow it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly provide remote image URL inputs to external apps. The privacy risk depends on user data and provider controls, so the risk is medium.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detector reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the evidence is Markdown code formatting and documented shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown inline code or documentation tables. There is no Ruby source file, interpolation, or executable backtick expression.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography blocker at line 3 is descriptive metadata about image generation models, not cryptographic code or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains a natural language description of model names and capabilities. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm selection is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to public service documentation, an image asset, and placeholder image inputs. They are not evidence of covert exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or example references tied to the stated inference.sh workflow. No hidden endpoint or credential transfer was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access for belt Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash access scoped to belt. This is narrower than unrestricted shell access, but it still allows external CLI operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration is clear and scoped. The risk is real because belt operations can contact external services.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Model Invocation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation repeatedly instructs users to run hosted image model applications with prompt or image input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are direct command invocations for external providers. This matches the skill purpose but requires user awareness.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with Model CLIs
content.value_statement: AI image tools often require different model names, inputs, and commands. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical prompts for inference.sh image workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to run inference.sh image apps through the belt CLI.
Lists supported model app IDs for GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and upscaling.
Provides examples for text-to-image, image editing, image upscaling, and image stitching.
Includes a command to browse available image apps by category.
Points users to related skills for platform, model-specific, upscaling, background removal, and video workflows.
limitations: Requires the belt CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account.
Generated outputs depend on third-party model availability, pricing, and provider policies.
Prompts and image URLs may be sent to external services.
The skill documents commands but does not add local image generation code.

use_cases.title: Product Visual Prototyping
use_cases.description: Create product photo concepts, packaging ideas, and style variants for review before production work.

use_cases.title: Marketing Asset Drafting
use_cases.description: Generate campaign images, social graphics, and headline poster concepts with multiple image models.

use_cases.title: Developer Workflow Testing
use_cases.description: Test image model endpoints and compare model behavior through repeatable CLI commands.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AI image generation skill to create a studio product photo of a ceramic mug on a clean background.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose a model for a specific goal
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an image model for accurate text in a poster, then prepare the belt command I should run.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an image editing command that changes the background of my product image to a beach at sunset.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Step Image Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CLI workflow to generate three product concepts, upscale the best image, and stitch comparison outputs horizontally.

output_examples.input: Create a clean product image for a sneaker launch.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a high-quality product model, writes a belt command, and explains which prompt details improve lighting and composition.

output_examples.input: I need a poster with readable text.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects a text-focused image model, suggests concise poster wording, and prepares a command for generation.

output_examples.input: Upscale this image and make a comparison strip.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an upscaling step, then a stitching step that combines the original and enhanced image for review.

best_practices: Use non-sensitive prompts and image URLs unless your provider agreement permits private data processing.
Specify model goals such as realism, text accuracy, speed, cost, or resolution before generating images.
Review provider pricing and output rights before using generated assets in production.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential images or unreleased product designs without approval.
Do not assume every listed model supports the same parameters or output quality.
Do not run generation commands repeatedly without checking cost and rate limits.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant prepare belt CLI commands for AI image generation, editing, upscaling, and stitching.

faq.question: Does it generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses inference.sh hosted apps through the belt CLI.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: You need the belt CLI installed and an authenticated inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can I use private images?
faq.answer: Only use private images when your organization and provider terms allow external processing.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs external CLI commands and can send prompts or image URLs to hosted model providers.
